name: Userfront Authentication API
what_it_does: Multi-tenant authentication and user management platform with REST API — handling signup, login, JWTs, MFA, and user data for SaaS applications with tenant isolation built in, supporting multiple authentication methods and providing user management dashboard.
use_cases: Adding multi-tenant authentication to SaaS apps with per-tenant user isolation
Managing users, roles, and tenants programmatically via Management API
Implementing passwordless authentication (magic links, passkeys) alongside password login
Syncing user data and custom attributes across tenants via REST API
Building auth flows for B2B SaaS without complex self-hosted identity infrastructure
not_for: Enterprise SSO with SAML/SCIM at scale (use Okta or Auth0 Enterprise)
Consumer identity at massive scale without B2B organization needs
Teams needing advanced fraud detection or anomaly detection in auth flows
best_when: A B2B SaaS team needs multi-tenant auth with straightforward API, generous free tier, and built-in tenant isolation without Auth0's pricing.
avoid_when: You need enterprise SSO, complex RBAC governance, or proven scale beyond hundreds of thousands of users.

auth.notes: Workspace API key for server-side calls. Short-lived JWTs issued for user sessions. Admin token for management operations. Tenant-scoped access control. No fine-grained OAuth2 scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 10,000 MAU, multi-tenant support
pricing.notes: Generous free tier with multi-tenant support — uncommon at this price point. US-based. Bootstrapped company. Focus on B2B SaaS with tenant isolation. Simpler and more affordable than Auth0 for early-stage products.

performance.rate_limits: Standard rate limits; documentation recommends caching

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers users, tenants, roles, and session management. JWT validation for user tokens. Webhook support for auth events. SDK-first but API is well-documented. Smaller ecosystem than Auth0 — fewer community resources.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation idempotent by email. User updates idempotent. Role assignments idempotent. Token issuance creates new tokens — not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Tenant context required for most operations — must specify tenantId in requests
JWT expiry short (1 hour default) — must implement refresh token flow in agents
Role system is basic compared to full RBAC platforms — limited to predefined roles
MFA setup requires user interaction — cannot be fully automated via API

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. JWT-based sessions with short expiry. Tenant isolation by design. API keys server-side only. US data residency. Bcrypt password hashing.
